raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should the help focus on prose-level editing only, or on higher-level argument/structure and claim support as well?","rationale":{"R1.1":"A broad request for help with a paper section can centrally include reframing the contribution and positioning of RoboLikert.","R1.2":"The section makes a connected argument from evaluation gaps to the need for crowdsourced human judgment, so checking logical consistency is centrally relevant.","R1.3":"The section relies on reported correlations, agreement scores, and scale claims to support its conclusions, making evidence-claim alignment central.","R1.5":"Because the delegation targets an entire section, section-level narrative order and arc are centrally in scope.","R1.6":"A generic request for help with a section commonly includes sentence- and paragraph-level polish.","RX.1":"Any projection should preserve uncertainty about the underspecified intent of the delegation.","RX.2":"The projection must guard against over-reading the broad request as a demand for unsupported novelty or empirical validation work.","RX.3":"The projection must stay within responsibility assignment rather than editing or evaluating the artifact itself.","RX.4":"The section concerns human evaluation of robotics, so downstream-harm avoidance remains active for claims about safety and scalable evaluation.","RX.5":"The projection should remain traceable to explicit features of the delegation and artifact."}}

parsed.clarification_question: Should the help focus on prose-level editing only, or on higher-level argument/structure and claim support as well?

rationale.R1.1: A broad request for help with a paper section can centrally include reframing the contribution and positioning of RoboLikert.
rationale.R1.2: The section makes a connected argument from evaluation gaps to the need for crowdsourced human judgment, so checking logical consistency is centrally relevant.
rationale.R1.3: The section relies on reported correlations, agreement scores, and scale claims to support its conclusions, making evidence-claim alignment central.
rationale.R1.5: Because the delegation targets an entire section, section-level narrative order and arc are centrally in scope.
rationale.R1.6: A generic request for help with a section commonly includes sentence- and paragraph-level polish.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection should preserve uncertainty about the underspecified intent of the delegation.
rationale.RX.2: The projection must guard against over-reading the broad request as a demand for unsupported novelty or empirical validation work.
rationale.RX.3: The projection must stay within responsibility assignment rather than editing or evaluating the artifact itself.
rationale.RX.4: The section concerns human evaluation of robotics, so downstream-harm avoidance remains active for claims about safety and scalable evaluation.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should remain traceable to explicit features of the delegation and artifact.
